food_subgroup: Fusion snack platter — spiced paneer on tortilla chips

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic nacho food product class (FOODON_00002473). No specific class exists for paneer tikka nachos fusion variant; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers savoury snacks broadly. No specific code exists for Indian-Mexican fusion nachos; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for paneer tikka nachos or this fusion snack category.

main_ingredients: tikka marinade (yoghurt, spices, chilli, ginger, garlic)

serving.natural_language_portion: One sharing plate of paneer tikka nachos weighing approximately 350 g, typically served as a starter or snack for one to two people.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: USDA FoodData Central — Tortilla chips (plain, salted) SR Legacy #19056; Paneer (Indian cottage cheese) ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods; Tikka marinade and toppings estimated from USDA SR Legacy ingredient entries
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for paneer tikka nachos exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: approximately 55% tortilla chips (USDA SR Legacy #19056), 30% paneer tikka (ICMR-NIN paneer + tikka spice blend), 15% toppings (salsa, sour cream, jalapeños, coriander). Values represent a plausible central estimate for a restaurant-style serving. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Tortilla chips GI analogous estimate — Corn chips GI ~72 (Atkinson et al. 2008); composite dish GI estimated at ~55 after protein and fat dilution from paneer and dairy toppings
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for paneer tikka nachos. Corn/tortilla chips have a published GI of approximately 72 (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283). The composite dish GI is estimated at approximately 55 (low) after accounting for the fat and protein contribution of paneer, sour cream, and cheese toppings, which are known to attenuate postprandial glycaemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 112 g carbohydrate in 350 g serving) / 100 = 61.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1470 mg per sharing plate)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid paneer tikka nachos due to the high sodium load from salted tortilla chips, tikka spice blends, and added sauces, which can significantly contribute to daily sodium intake and elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) but high glycemic load per serving (~61.6) from refined corn tortilla chips
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume paneer tikka nachos in small portions only, as the high glycemic load per sharing plate can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation despite the moderating effect of paneer protein and dairy fat.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from paneer and dairy toppings (~6.2 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit paneer tikka nachos due to the combined burden of saturated fat from paneer and cheese, elevated sodium from chips and sauces, and the calorie-dense nature of the dish, all of which may adversely affect lipid profiles and blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Paneer Tikka Nachos is an Indian-Mexican fusion dish served in Singapore, combining the bold spiced flavours of North Indian paneer tikka with the crunchy, shareable format of Mexican-style nachos. The dish consists of a base of salted corn tortilla chips topped with cubes of paneer (Indian fresh cottage cheese) that have been marinated in a tikka spice blend — typically yoghurt, cumin, coriander, turmeric, chilli, ginger, and garlic — and then grilled or pan-seared. The nachos are finished with accompaniments such as tomato salsa, sour cream or mint-yoghurt raita, sliced jalapeños, melted cheese, and fresh coriander. The result is a dish that is simultaneously crispy, creamy, spicy, and tangy. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense at approximately 310 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein contribution from paneer (~9.5 g per 100 g) and significant fat from both the fried chips and dairy components (~16.5 g per 100 g). Sodium is high (~420 mg per 100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low), but the glycemic load per sharing plate is high (~61.6), which is clinically relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is vegetarian and typically halal, making it suitable for a broad Singapore dining audience. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary staple.
common_user_queries: How many calories in paneer tikka nachos?
What is the GI of nachos with paneer?
Can diabetics eat paneer tikka nachos?

Where can I find paneer tikka nachos in Singapore?
Is paneer tikka nachos gluten free?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for paneer tikka nachos exists in any approved primary nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA SR Legacy (tortilla chips) and ICMR-NIN (paneer). GI is an analogous estimate based on published corn chip GI values adjusted for the protein and fat dilution effect of paneer and dairy toppings. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved databases and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated; no single approved source entry covers this fusion dish. Values should be reviewed if a direct laboratory analysis becomes available. 2. Vitamin D is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported for this dish type in the component sources used (USDA SR Legacy and ICMR-NIN do not provide vitamin D for tortilla chips or paneer in the relevant entries). 3. Selenium is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used for this estimation. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy fat profile for tortilla chips and ICMR-NIN paneer fat composition; values are plausible but carry medium uncertainty. 5. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous corn chip GI of ~72 (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) attenuated by paneer protein and dairy fat; glycemic load per 350 g serving = (55 × 112) / 100 = 61.6. 6. The input food_name 'PANNER TIKKA NACHOS' contains a common misspelling; canonical English name corrected to 'Paneer Tikka Nachos'. 7. This dish is served at Two Amigos / Fiesta Mexico restaurants in Singapore and represents a niche fusion menu item. 8. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the dish is served in Singapore.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Corrected food_name spelling from 'PANNER' to 'Paneer'. Built full component-estimated nutrient profile from USDA SR Legacy and ICMR-NIN. Added GI analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008. Added multilingual names for Singapore context. Added ontology with closest_parent codes. Added full health context for all three required conditions. Added LLM description >200 characters.

Updated `gi_source.source_match_notes` and `health_context.type2_diabetes.key_factor` to reflect this change. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Verified -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Verified no zero core macros. Verified ontology match_type values are within permitted set. Confirmed PASS on all hard gates and Q1-Q8.
